frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: neural tuning optimized to environmental stimulus frequency, with cardinal-orientation prior

frameworks.name: neural population model of working memory
frameworks.one_line: memory neurons' tuning functions made stimulus-frequency dependent

frameworks.name: slot-based working memory model
frameworks.one_line: discrete-slot competitor unable to capture stimulus-specific recall variation

core_question: Can stimulus-specific biases and variations in orientation working-memory recall be explained by efficient coding of neural tuning to natural stimulus statistics?
key_assumptions: neural tuning functions adapt to environmental stimulus frequencies
natural orientation statistics favor cardinal over oblique orientations
working memory recall errors reflect underlying neural population coding
prior expectations shape encoding precision and bias
manipulations: target orientation across the full circular range
nontarget distribution shape (bimodal congruent vs incongruent with cardinal axes)
key_findings: efficient-coding model captures set-size effects and stimulus-specific recall variations better than slot-based competitor
pre-adaptation responses were repulsed away from cardinal axes
exposure to incongruent (oblique-peaked) distribution produced systematic decreases in cardinal repulsion
adaptation-induced shifts persisted after the adaptation blocks
interpretations: stimulus-specific recall effects arise from efficient-coding tuning shaped by environmental priors
prior expectation about stimulus distribution causally generates the repulsive bias pattern
